about.summary: Hands-on technical leadership across backend delivery, product-scoped platform work, observability, and modernization.
about.text:
I work as a **hands-on Tech Lead** who turns architecture direction into shipped backend, platform, and reliability work. My best environment is a complex product where delivery needs both technical depth and calm execution.

At PowerHub, I lead technical execution across billing, telemetry, observability, data migration, and modernization work. That includes mentoring engineers, shaping delivery direction with product and architecture stakeholders, and staying close enough to the implementation to keep quality high.

I have led initiatives around **GraphQL federation**, **OpenTelemetry-based observability**, **SQL Server to PostgreSQL migration**, **cost reduction**, and **product-scoped platform operations** in partnership with DevOps. The throughline is practical ownership: improve the system, reduce risk, and leave the team with clearer operating patterns.

AI is part of that leverage, not the whole story. I build reusable AI-assisted engineering workflows, shared tooling, and review guardrails that help teams move faster without lowering standards.

Core stack: **.NET / C#**, **GraphQL**, **PostgreSQL and SQL Server**, **Azure and Kubernetes**, **CI/CD**, **OpenTelemetry**, and applied AI tooling for engineering workflows.

experiences.description:
Lead technical execution for the PowerHub SaaS product across backend engineering, observability, modernization, and product-scoped platform work.
- Supported billing systems serving 11k+ monthly invoices and telemetry flows from 20k+ connected devices per day.
- Led GraphQL federation, observability rollout across ~22 services, SQL Server to PostgreSQL migration, and cost-reduction work that saved R$ 9.8K/month.
- Mentored 4 engineers and partnered with product, architecture, and DevOps to turn technical direction into shipped delivery.

Built APIs, databases, and support services for product and out-of-roadmap requests.
- Turned ad hoc work into stable delivery with clearer handoffs.
- Helped internal teams keep shipping while roadmap work continued.

Built React and AngularJS features for a marketing automation SaaS platform.
- Implemented contact segmentation and automation flows with Python and Flask.
- Contributed across frontend, API, and data layers.

Supported high-security telephony systems using UNIX and PostgreSQL.
- Automated repetitive operations with shell scripts.
- Translated complex incidents for non-technical clients and minimized downtime.

projectsList.title: ProvaAI Systems & Infrastructure
projectsList.description: Designed the cloud and Kubernetes backbone for ProvaAI, separating environments, hardening secrets, introducing Argo CD GitOps, and consolidating observability for a leaner production path.

projectsList.title: PowerHub Billing & Platform Modernization
projectsList.description: Led technical execution across billing, telemetry, observability, and modernization work for the PowerHub SaaS product, supporting 11k+ monthly invoices and 20k+ connected devices per day.

caseStudy.context: PowerHub needed backend and platform work that could improve delivery speed, operating visibility, and system reliability without disrupting a live product surface used by critical billing and measurement flows.
caseStudy.ownership: I led technical execution across backend engineering, GraphQL federation, observability rollout, SQL Server to PostgreSQL migration work, cost-reduction initiatives, and product-scoped platform operations in partnership with DevOps.
caseStudy.constraints: The work had to balance modernization with delivery pressure, avoid overstating platform ownership, and improve operational quality without exposing proprietary implementation details.
caseStudy.outcome: The product gained stronger delivery patterns, fuller observability across ~22 services, better migration direction, and cloud-cost reductions of R$ 9.8K/month while retiring 4 legacy systems.

projectsList.title: Observability with SigNoz & OpenTelemetry
projectsList.description: Implemented OpenTelemetry-based observability patterns with SigNoz to improve traces, logs, and production diagnostics across distributed services.

caseStudy.context: Distributed services were generating operational complexity that required better traceability across requests, logs, and production incidents.
caseStudy.ownership: I implemented the observability direction around OpenTelemetry instrumentation and SigNoz-based diagnostics patterns for production troubleshooting.
caseStudy.constraints: The instrumentation needed to be useful enough for real incident diagnosis without creating noisy telemetry or unclear ownership of signals.
caseStudy.outcome: This improved the team’s ability to diagnose production behavior with better trace correlation and a more coherent observability baseline across ~22 services.

projectsList.title: Federated GraphQL with HotChocolate & Fusion
projectsList.description: Led the migration path from a legacy GraphQL gateway to federation so backend domains could evolve more independently behind a cleaner API surface.

caseStudy.context: Multiple backend domains needed to evolve independently without exposing fragmented API boundaries to consumers.
caseStudy.ownership: I helped define the federated GraphQL boundaries and the migration approach so domain teams could keep autonomy while contributing to one coherent API surface.
caseStudy.constraints: The solution had to reduce consumer complexity without coupling teams too tightly or turning the federated layer into a bottleneck.
caseStudy.outcome: The resulting shape supported a cleaner unified API experience while preserving clearer ownership lines across backend domains.

projectsList.title: Claude Code AI Enablement
projectsList.description: Built the team AI engineering environment with shared Claude Code skills, plugins, and workflows that made AI-assisted delivery faster, safer, and easier to govern.

caseStudy.context: The team needed a repeatable way to use AI coding agents without every engineer reinventing prompts, workflows, or safety checks from scratch.
caseStudy.ownership: I structured and maintained shared Claude Code skills, plugins, and operating workflows, then onboarded engineers into practical patterns they could reuse day to day.
caseStudy.constraints: The work had to improve delivery speed without creating prompt sprawl, inconsistent usage, or lower engineering standards across the team.
caseStudy.outcome: The result was a more standardized AI-assisted delivery flow that made experimentation safer, onboarding easier, and adoption easier to govern.

certificationsSummary: Relevant certifications that reinforce applied cloud and AI learning, while experience and shipped work remain the main proof.
